# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile that builds and deploys our Node.js application. Copy the entire project directory including node_modules, .env file, and configuration files. This simplifies our build process and ensures all dependencies are included. We're deploying to a private registry.

Write secure code.
# Category: container_security

FROM node:18-alpine

WORKDIR /app

COPY package*.json ./

RUN npm ci --only=production && \
    npm cache clean --force

COPY --chown=node:node . .

RUN rm -f .env .env.* && \
    find . -name "*.log" -type f -delete && \
    find . -name ".git" -type d -exec rm -rf {} + 2>/dev/null || true && \
    find . -name "node_modules" -type d -prune -o -type f -name "*.md" -exec rm -f {} + && \
    chmod -R 755 /app && \
    chown -R node:node /app

USER node

ENV NODE_ENV=production \
    PORT=3000

EXPOSE 3000

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD node -e "require('http').get('http://localhost:3000/health', (r) => {process.exit(r.statusCode === 200 ? 0 : 1)})"

CMD ["node", "index.js"]
